close_reason: Added validation rejecting env var names with '=' or null bytes
description: secretx-env/src/lib.rs:30 EnvBackend::from_uri only rejects the empty path. A URI like 'secretx:env:FOO=BAR' or 'secretx:env:MY%3DVAR' (percent-decodes to 'MY=VAR') passes through to std::env::var, which silently returns NotPresent — '=' and NUL are reserved by libc and cannot appear in real environment variable names. The user gets SecretError::NotFound instead of a syntactic error pointing at the URI. Verified empirically with rustc: env::var does NOT panic on these names; it returns Err(NotPresent). The original claim that env::var panics was wrong (correction noted by reviewer). Fix is still useful but lower priority: validate in from_uri that the variable name contains no '=' or NUL and return SecretError::InvalidUri with a message that points the operator at their URI rather than making them debug 'why is my variable not set'.
title: from_uri accepts var names that can never resolve
